author: kamael0909
title: Thread-safety violation: failures set mutated without lock in parallel execution
notes: The claim is factually incorrect. The `failures` set is only ever accessed from the main thread. Worker threads run `_run_parallel_task`, which does NOT receive `failures` as a parameter — it only receives `progress_failures` and `started_at` (both lock-protected). `_queue_parallel_tasks` runs in the main thread (sequential loop), and `_complete_parallel_future` runs in the main thread via `_drain_parallel_completions` (consuming futures with as_completed). There is no concurrent access to `failures`. S024 by @jasonsutter87 makes the same incorrect claim.
